title: carex contigua subsp. leersii
intro: Carex contigua subsp. leersii, also known as Leers' sedge, is a type of perennial plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, Leers' sedge has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. Leers' sedge can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
